The most likely correct answer is **\boxed{H}**.

Voltaire’s letter directly critiques the cruelty of nature and human suffering, questioning the idea of a "best of possible worlds" (a central theme in his satire *Candide*). While he was an Enlightenment figure, his skepticism of reason’s ability to explain suffering and his emphasis on emotional and moral outrage align with the early Romantic critique of Enlightenment rationalism. No other option as clearly reflects his specific response to the Lisbon earthquake and his broader philosophical stance.
